expressions.english: cause of failure
to fail
to fail in business
to end in failure
to fail in achieving one's goal
expressions.notes:
목표 = goal, aim, target
달성 = achievement, accomplishment

expressions.english: It's a failure again this time.
I have not failed once.
expressions.notes: -(으)ㄴ 적 없다 = to have never p.p.

expressions.english: Do not be afraid of failure.
expressions.notes: 두려워하다 = to be afraid of, scared of

expressions.english: I failed my diet again.
expressions.notes:
다이어트 = diet; losing one's weight by putting in effort
또 = again

expressions.english: It is natural to fail in the beginning.
expressions.notes:
처음 = first; for the first time
-기(이) 당연하다 = to be natural to + V; to be reasonable to + V

expressions.english: I thought I would succeed, but I failed.
expressions.notes:
성공하다 = to succeed
-(으)ㄹ 줄 알았는데 = I thought + S + was going to be + adjective, but...; I thought + S + would + V +, but...

expressions.english: What do you think the reason is for failing?

expressions.korean: 오늘 처음으로 빵을 만들어 봤는데 실패했어요.
expressions.english: I tried making bread for the first time, but I failed.
expressions.notes:
처음으로 = for the first time
빵 = bread
만들다 = to make

expressions.korean: 실패할 걱정을 많이 하면 시작도 할 수 가 없어요.
expressions.english: If you worry a lot about failing, then you cannot even start.
expressions.notes:
걱정을 하다 = to worry
시작하다 = to start, begin

expressions.korean: 이번 프로젝트는 성공할 줄 알았는데 또 실패로 끝났어요.
expressions.english: I thought I would succeed at this project, but it ended up a failure again.

expressions.korean: 이번 학기에는 학점 4.3을 받는 게 목표 였는데 실패했어요.
expressions.english: My goal was to get a 4.3 GPA this semester, but I failed.
expressions.notes:
학기 = semester
학점 = school credits; grade (in school)
받다 = to receive

expressions.korean: 올해 초에 세운 다섯 가지 목표 중 두 개는 벌써 실패했어요.
expressions.english: I already failed in (achieving) two goals out of the five I set in the beginning of this year.
expressions.notes:
올해 = this year
초 = beginning of
세우다 = to set up; to make something stand
목표를 세우다 = to make a goal
다섯 = five (native Korean number)
가지 = kind (of)
중 = among; between; out of
두 = two (native Korean number)
뛰 is the adjective form of 둘.
개 is a general counter for inanimate objects.

expressions.korean: 유명 배우들이 많이 나왔는데도 그 영화는 흥행에 실패했어요.
expressions.english:
The movie failed at the box office even though a lot of famous actors starred in it.
That movie was a flop even though there were many famous actors in it.
expressions.notes:
유명 배우 = famous actor/actress
-들 is a suffix used to indicate plural.
나오다 = to come out
흥행 = box office hit

expressions.korean: 자희 회사에서 오랫동안 준비한 프로젝트가 실패로 돌아갔어요.
expressions.english:
The project that our company prepared for a long time was a failure.
The project, which our company spent a long time preparing, ended up a failure.
expressions.notes:
자희 = (polite) we; us; our
오랫동안 = for a long time
준비하다 = to prepare; to get ready

expressions.korean: 오늘 아침에는 일찍 일어나서 운동을 하려고 했는데 늦잠을 자서 실패했어요.
expressions.english: I was going to wake up early and exercise this morning, but I overslept and (ultimately) failed.
expressions.notes:
아침 = morning; breakfast
일찍 = early; soon
일어나다 = to wake up; to get up
운동 = exercise; sports
운동을 하다 = to exercise, work out; to play a sport
-(으)려고 하다 = to plan to + V; to think of + V-ing; to mean to + V
늦잠을 자다 = to get up late, oversleep, sleep in
